speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: Come, come. They are almost here. How

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: many stand for consulships?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: Three, they say; but ’tis thought of

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: everyone Coriolanus will carry it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: That’s a brave fellow, but he’s vengeance

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: proud and loves not the common people.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: ’Faith, there hath been many great

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: men that have flattered the people who ne’er loved

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: them; and there be many that they have loved they

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: know not wherefore; so that, if they love they

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: know not why, they hate upon no better a ground.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: Therefore, for Coriolanus neither to care whether

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: they love or hate him manifests the true knowledge

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: he has in their disposition and, out of his noble

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: carelessness, lets them plainly see ’t.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: If he did not care whether he had their

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: love or no, he waved indifferently ’twixt doing them

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: neither good nor harm; but he seeks their hate with

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: greater devotion than they can render it him and

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: leaves nothing undone that may fully discover him

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: their opposite. Now, to seem to affect the malice

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: and displeasure of the people is as bad as that

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: which he dislikes, to flatter them for their love.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: He hath deserved worthily of his

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: country, and his ascent is not by such easy degrees

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: as those who, having been supple and courteous to

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: the people, bonneted, without any further deed to

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: have them at all into their estimation and report;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: but he hath so planted his honors in their eyes and

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: his actions in their hearts that for their tongues to

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: be silent and not confess so much were a kind of

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: ingrateful injury. To report otherwise were a malice

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: that, giving itself the lie, would pluck reproof

speaker:
SECOND
               
              OFFICER
text: and rebuke from every ear that heard it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: No more of him; he’s a worthy man.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: Make way. They are coming.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Having determined of the Volsces and

speaker: MENENIUS
text: To send for Titus Lartius, it remains,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: As the main point of this our after-meeting,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: To gratify his noble service that

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Hath thus stood for his country. Therefore please you,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Most reverend and grave elders, to desire

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The present consul and last general

speaker: MENENIUS
text: In our well-found successes to report

speaker: MENENIUS
text: A little of that worthy work performed

speaker: MENENIUS
text: By Martius Caius Coriolanus, whom

speaker: MENENIUS
text: We met here both to thank and to remember

speaker: MENENIUS
text: With honors like himself.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Speak, good Cominius.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Leave nothing out for length, and make us think

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Rather our state’s defective for requital,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text:
Than we to stretch it out.
                 
                (
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Tribunes
                .
                )
              Masters o’ th’ people,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: We do request your kindest ears and, after,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Your loving motion toward the common body

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: To yield what passes here.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: We are convented

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Upon a pleasing treaty and have hearts

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Inclinable to honor and advance

speaker: SICINIUS
text: The theme of our assembly.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Which the rather

speaker: BRUTUS
text: We shall be blest to do if he remember

speaker: BRUTUS
text: A kinder value of the people than

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He hath hereto prized them at.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That’s off, that’s off!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I would you rather had been silent. Please you

speaker: MENENIUS
text: To hear Cominius speak?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Most willingly,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But yet my caution was more pertinent

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Than the rebuke you give it.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: He loves your people,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: But tie him not to be their bedfellow.—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Worthy Cominius, speak.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Nay, keep your place.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Sit, Coriolanus. Never shame to hear

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: What you have nobly done.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Your Honors, pardon.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I had rather have my wounds to heal again

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Than hear say how I got them.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Sir, I hope

speaker: BRUTUS
text: My words disbenched you not?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: No, sir. Yet oft,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: When blows have made me stay, I fled from words.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: You soothed not, therefore hurt not; but your people,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I love them as they weigh.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Pray now, sit down.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I had rather have one scratch my head i’ th’ sun

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: When the alarum were struck than idly sit

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To hear my nothings monstered.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Masters of the people,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Your multiplying spawn how can he flatter—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That’s thousand to one good one—when you now see

speaker: MENENIUS
text: He had rather venture all his limbs for honor

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Than one on ’s ears to hear it.—Proceed, Cominius.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: I shall lack voice. The deeds of Coriolanus

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Should not be uttered feebly. It is held

speaker: COMINIUS
text: That valor is the chiefest virtue and

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Most dignifies the haver; if it be,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The man I speak of cannot in the world

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Be singly counterpoised. At sixteen years,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: When Tarquin made a head for Rome, he fought

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Beyond the mark of others. Our then dictator,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Whom with all praise I point at, saw him fight

speaker: COMINIUS
text: When with his Amazonian chin he drove

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The bristled lips before him. He bestrid

speaker: COMINIUS
text: An o’erpressed Roman and i’ th’ Consul’s view

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Slew three opposers. Tarquin’s self he met

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And struck him on his knee. In that day’s feats,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: When he might act the woman in the scene,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He proved best man i’ th’ field and for his meed

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Was brow-bound with the oak. His pupil age

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Man-entered thus, he waxèd like a sea,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And in the brunt of seventeen battles since

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He lurched all swords of the garland. For this last,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Before and in Corioles, let me say,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: I cannot speak him home. He stopped the flyers

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And by his rare example made the coward

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Turn terror into sport. As weeds before

speaker: COMINIUS
text: A vessel under sail, so men obeyed

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And fell below his stem. His sword, Death’s stamp,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Where it did mark, it took; from face to foot

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He was a thing of blood, whose every motion

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Was timed with dying cries. Alone he entered

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The mortal gate o’ th’ city, which he painted

speaker: COMINIUS
text: With shunless destiny; aidless came off

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And with a sudden reinforcement struck

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Corioles like a planet. Now all’s his,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: When by and by the din of war gan pierce

speaker: COMINIUS
text: His ready sense; then straight his doubled spirit

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Requickened what in flesh was fatigate,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And to the battle came he, where he did

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Run reeking o’er the lives of men as if

speaker: COMINIUS
text: ’Twere a perpetual spoil; and till we called

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Both field and city ours, he never stood

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To ease his breast with panting.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Worthy man!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: He cannot but with measure fit the honors

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Which we devise him.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Our spoils he kicked at

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And looked upon things precious as they were

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The common muck of the world. He covets less

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Than misery itself would give, rewards

speaker: COMINIUS
text: His deeds with doing them, and is content

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To spend the time to end it.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: He’s right noble.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Let him be called for.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Call Coriolanus.

speaker: OFFICER
text: He doth appear.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The Senate, Coriolanus, are well pleased

speaker: MENENIUS
text: To make thee consul.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I do owe them still

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My life and services.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: It then remains

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That you do speak to the people.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I do beseech you,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let me o’erleap that custom, for I cannot

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Put on the gown, stand naked, and entreat them

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: For my wounds’ sake to give their suffrage. Please you

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That I may pass this doing.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Sir, the people

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Must have their voices; neither will they bate

speaker: SICINIUS
text: One jot of ceremony.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Put them not to ’t.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Pray you, go fit you to the custom, and

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Take to you, as your predecessors have,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Your honor with your form.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: It is a part

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That I shall blush in acting, and might well

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Be taken from the people.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Mark you that?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To brag unto them “Thus I did, and thus!”

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Show them th’ unaching scars, which I should hide,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As if I had received them for the hire

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of their breath only!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Do not stand upon ’t.—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: We recommend to you, tribunes of the people,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Our purpose to them, and to our noble consul

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Wish we all joy and honor.

speaker: SENATORS
text: To Coriolanus come all joy and honor!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: You see how he intends to use the people.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: May they perceive ’s intent! He will require them

speaker: SICINIUS
text: As if he did contemn what he requested

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Should be in them to give.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Come, we’ll inform them

text: Of our proceedings here. On th’ marketplace
